title: Short conversational answer
rawText: Yep, that looks right. The backend should keep the queue local, and the tray can ask for the next speakable item when it is ready.

content.text: Yep, that looks right. The backend should keep the queue local, and the tray can ask for the next speakable item when it is ready.

title: Answer with diff
rawText:
The settings default needs one small change:

```diff
-  readBehavior: 'auto',
+  readBehavior: 'manual',
```

Manual is the safer initial behavior because it waits for user intent.

content.text:
The settings default needs one small change:

```diff

+  readBehavior: 'manual',
```

Manual is the safer initial behavior because it waits for user intent.

title: Answer with logs
rawText:
The backend started, but the health check failed after the port bind:

```text
[2026-06-20T20:41:12.011Z] INFO server listening on 127.0.0.1:4777
[2026-06-20T20:41:12.215Z] ERROR health check failed: ECONNREFUSED
    at connect (node:net:1090:16)
```

I would check whether the probe is using the same host and port as the server.

title: Answer with table
rawText:
Here is the current queue state:

| Status | Count |
| --- | ---: |
| pending | 2 |
| playing | 1 |
| played | 4 |

The important part is that only one item should be marked playing at a time.

title: Answer with JSON
rawText:
The settings payload should look like this:

```json
{
  "readBehavior": "manual",
  "muted": false,
  "rate": 1,
  "volume": 0.8
}
```

Those defaults keep playback predictable for a first run.

title: Answer with Spoken and Displayed sections
rawText:
Spoken:
The backend queue is ready, and the next step is to connect it to the local hook command.

Displayed:
- Queue shape: id, raw source, speakable text, source app, status, timestamps.
- Playback states: pending, playing, played, skipped.
- Persistence target: %APPDATA%\Agent Hotline\speech-queue.json.
